message:
fix(convert): three-pass audit of fnx-convert

Multi-pass audit of fnx-convert (~1035 lines, EdgeListPayload /
AdjacencyPayload converters across Graph + DiGraph + MultiGraph +
MultiDiGraph). Found and fixed six real defects across three
passes; the existing 11-test suite expanded to 14 with new
regression tests pinning the fixes.

Pass 1 — public API symmetry
----------------------------
``to_normalized_payload`` previously only handled the undirected
``Graph`` case, leaving callers with ``DiGraph`` / ``MultiGraph``
/ ``MultiDiGraph`` to call ``.snapshot()`` directly. Add three
parallel public functions so the API is symmetric:

- ``digraph_to_normalized_payload`` → ``DiGraphSnapshot``
- ``multigraph_to_normalized_payload`` → ``MultiGraphSnapshot``
- ``multidigraph_to_normalized_payload`` → ``MultiDiGraphSnapshot``

Plus the underlying ``DiGraphSnapshot`` / ``MultiGraphSnapshot`` /
``MultiDiGraphSnapshot`` types are now imported from fnx-classes
(they had been added to the snapshot family earlier this round).

Pass 2 — serde deserialization defaults
---------------------------------------
``EdgeListPayload`` and ``AdjacencyPayload`` previously required
their primary fields (``edges`` / ``adjacency``), so an empty
JSON object ``{}`` failed with "missing field". Add
``#[serde(default)]`` to:

- ``EdgeListPayload.edges`` so ``{"nodes": ["a"]}`` (isolated
  nodes only) deserializes
- ``AdjacencyPayload.adjacency`` so ``{}`` deserializes as an
  empty graph

Combined with the existing default on ``EdgeListPayload.nodes``,
both payload shapes now accept the empty-graph degenerate case.

Pass 3 — dispatch-failure audit trail
--------------------------------------
When the backend dispatcher rejected a conversion request, the
converter returned the error directly without leaving an
evidence-ledger entry, so the audit trail had no record of the
rejection. Add ``record_dispatch_failure`` helper and wire it
into all 8 ``dispatch.resolve`` call sites
(from_edge_list / digraph / multigraph / multidigraph + adjacency
counterparts) via a small ``if let Err(err) = &resolve`` pattern.

Tests
-----
- ``empty_payload_deserializes_via_serde_default`` — pins the
  Pass-2 fix across both payload types and several mixed shapes.
- ``to_normalized_payload_variants_match_underlying_snapshot`` —
  pins the Pass-1 API additions, asserting each variant defers
  to ``graph.snapshot()`` exactly.
- ``successful_conversion_records_decision_in_ledger`` — pins
  the audit-trail invariant: any conversion (success or failure)
  produces at least one decision in the ledger.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
